title: Timing synchronization of 5g v2x sidelink transmissions
abstract: Methods and apparatus, including computer program products, are provided for timing synchronization of sidelinks. In some example embodiments, there may be provided an apparatus including at least one processor and at least one memory including computer program code, the at least one memory and the computer program code configured to, with the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to at least receive information to enable synchronized operation of a vehicle-to -vehicle sidelink with another user equipment; and configure the vehicle-to -vehicle sidelink. Related systems, methods, and articles of manufacture are also disclosed.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED 1. A method comprising: receiving, at a user equipment, information to enable synchronized operation of a vehicle- to-vehicle sidelink with another user equipment; and configuring, by the user equipment, the vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink is on a dedicated carrier to at least the other user equipment. 3. The method as in any of claims 1-2, wherein the vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink is on a shared carrier that is shared with at least a base station. 4. The method as in any of claims 1-3, wherein the information includes an indication regarding whether the vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink is on a shared carrier with an uplink to a base station. 5. The method as in any of claims 1-4, wherein the information includes an indication regarding whether a carrier is dedicated to the vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink. 6. The method as in any of claims 1-5, wherein the information includes an indication regarding whether one or more base stations operating in the carrier are synchronized. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the one or more base stations comprise all of the base stations operating in the carrier. 8. The method as in any of claims 1-7, wherein the information includes an indication regarding whether the base station being received by the user equipment is synchronized to a global navigation system timing source, a coordinated universal time, and/or a global positioning system timing source. 9. The method as in any of claims 1-8, wherein the information includes an indication regarding whether a plurality of the base stations including an evolved node B base station and a 5G node B base station have a common synchronization source. 10. The method as in any of claims 1-9 further comprising: receiving, from the base station, a location of the base station to enable the user equipment to compensate for timing error in network-based synchronization caused by propagation delay. 11. The method as in any of claims 1-10 further comprising: establishing the configured vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink. 12. An apparatus comprising: at least one processor; and at least one memory including computer program code, the at least one memory and the computer program code configured to, with the at least one processor, cause the apparatus to at least: receive information to enable synchronized operation of a vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink with another user equipment; and configure the vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink. 13. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink is on a dedicated carrier to at least the other user equipment. 14. The apparatus as in any of claims 12-13, wherein the vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink is on a shared carrier that is shared with at least a base station. 15. The apparatus as in any of claims 12-14, wherein the information includes an indication regarding whether the vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink is on a shared carrier with an uplink to a base station. 16. The apparatus as in any of claims 12-15, wherein the information includes an indication regarding whether a carrier is dedicated to the vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink. 17. The apparatus as in any of claims 12-17, wherein the one or more base stations comprise all of the base stations operating in the carrier. 19. The apparatus as in any of claims 12-19, wherein the information includes an indication regarding whether a plurality of the base stations including an evolved node B base station and a 5G node B base station have a common synchronization source. 21. The apparatus as in any of claims 12-20, wherein the apparatus is further caused to at least receive, from the base station, a location of the base station to enable the apparatus to compensate for timing error in network-based synchronization caused by propagation delay. 22. The apparatus as in any of claims 12-21, wherein the apparatus is further caused to at least establish the configured vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink. 23. The apparatus as in any of claims 12-22, wherein the apparatus comprises or is comprised in a user equipment. 24. An apparatus comprising: means for receiving information to enable synchronized operation of a vehicle-to -vehicle sidelink with another user equipment; and means for receiving subscription information for the user equipment. 25. The apparatus of claim 20 further comprising means for performing any of claims 2-11. 26. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium including program code which when executed by at least one processor causes operations comprising: receiving, at a user equipment, information to enable synchronized operation of a vehicle- to-vehicle sidelink with another user equipment; and configuring, by the user equipment, the vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink. 27. A method comprising: providing, by a base station, a radio access network to a user equipment configured to provide a vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink; and sending, by the base station, information to the user equipment, the information enabling synchronized operation of the vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink between the user equipment and another user equipment. 28. The method as in any of claims 27-32, wherein the information includes an indication regarding whether a plurality of the base stations including an evolved node B base station and a 5G node B base station have a common synchronization source. 34. The apparatus as in any of claims 35-37, wherein the information includes an indication regarding whether a carrier is dedicated to the vehicle-to-vehicle sidelink. 39.
